title: peucedanum officinale subsp. officinale
intro: Peucedanum officinale subsp. officinale, also known as wild carrot or Queen Anne's lace, is a perennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects. The plant's health benefits include alleviating menstrual cramps, reducing fever, and soothing digestive issues, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and seeds are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
